Article.ArticleTitle: The Relationship Between VO2 Max and Heart Rate Variability Parameters in Individuals with High Risk for Cardiovascular Disease.

Abstract.AbstractText: The purpose of this study was to evaluate the relationship between heart rate variability (HRV) parameters, classified into frequency and time domains, and maximal oxygen uptake (VO2 max) in individuals at high risk of cardiovascular disease but without a clinical diagnosis.

Based on a 10-year atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease risk score, 311 patients at high cardiovascular risk were included in this retrospective analysis. The study population was divided into tertiles according to VO2 max values. Demographic, clinical, echocardiographic, laboratory and HRV data were compared across tertiles. The associations between VO2 max and HRV parameters including standard deviation of normal-to-normal (NN) intervals over 24 hours, standard deviation of the averages of NN intervals in all 5-minute segments, standard deviation of NN intervals in 5-minute segments (SDNN index), root mean square of successive differences (rMSSD), percentage of successive NN intervals that differ by more than 50 ms, total power (TP), low frequency (LF), and high frequency (HF) were analyzed using Spearman correlation analysis.

VO2 max showed a moderate correlation with TP, LF and HF (rho = 0.211, p < 0.001; rho = 0.181, p = 0.001; and rho = 0.361, p < 0.001, respectively). In addition, significant correlations were found between VO2 max and rMSSD (rho = 0.122, p = 0.032) and SDNN index (rho = 0.184, p = 0.001). These associations remained statistically significant after adjusting for age using partial Spearman correlation analysis.
This study demonstrated significant correlations between HRV frequency and time domains with VO2 max in asymptomatic individuals with high cardiovascular risk.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Department of Rheumatology, Istanbul Goztepe Prof. Dr. Suleyman Yalcin City Hospital.
Department of Cardiology, Haydarpasa Sultan II. Abdulhamid Han Training and Research Hospital, Istanbul, Turkey.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: All the authors declare no conflict of interest.
